text: Assistant: Topic grouping IS present (index is grouped, every guide has a `topic:` field) — but the topics are degenerate: nearly one topic per guide ("build-system (1 guide)", "llm-configuration (1 guide)", etc.). The `topic` is basically echoing the slug rather than clustering. Let me see what the archeologist's routing actually does for topic assignment. [...] So "didn't organize by topic" could mean three quite different things, and the fix is very different for each. The ROUTE prompt in `capture.rs:1424` already tells the model to reuse existing topics from a full catalog — so the routing mechanism exists; the question is what outcome you expected. [...] Assistant: The advisor flags something decision-critical: **folders inherit the singleton problem.** 18 folders, most with one file, isn't "organized by topic" — it's flat with extra steps. And several singletons are obviously one cluster (embeddings + indexing + retrieval + vector-db ≈ retrieval/RAG). The real lever is `pc wiki doctor --retopic`, which already exists to collapse the flat wiki into broad topics. Let me check whether it has a preview mode before I run it on your live wiki. [...] Assistant: `--retopic` defaults to dry-run (no `--apply` = read-only, just prints the proposed taxonomy). Let me run it on your live wiki to see whether it collapses the ~18 singleton-ish topics into a handful of broad ones. This is the discriminating fact. [...] Assistant: This is the deciding fact. `--retopic` (dry-run) collapses your 27 guides into **4 clean broad topics** — exactly what "organized by topic" should look like: [...] So the root problem isn't missing folders — it's that **archeologist's per-session ROUTE mints fine-grained, near-singleton topics** (the "routing is the capture bottleneck" issue in your memory). The catalog starts empty on a fresh wiki, so each session invents narrow topics instead of clustering. `doctor --retopic` is the existing global consolidation pass that fixes it — and it needs **zero storage changes** (just re-stamps the `topic:` field; the index regroups). [...] Assistant: The archeologist diff is `--reset` + TUI counter work (peer stuff), not the topic logic. Topic assignment happens in the capture ROUTE step. Let me look at how ROUTE assigns topics and whether it reuses existing topics.
